skill.name: reproducibility-check
skill.description: Check whether a paper's Methods section contains all information needed for replication; use when preparing a manuscript for submission or reviewing methodological completeness.
skill.summary: Analyzes Methods section text against a structured checklist to identify missing replication-critical details, ambiguous descriptions, and reproducibility risks.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 35 static analysis findings from 4 scanned files (504 total lines). All 23 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives: the scanner misinterpreted markdown checkbox syntax `[ ]` and formatting brackets as cryptographic references in documentation files. All 10 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from markdown code fence backticks misidentified as shell commands. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings cite a legitimate GitHub source repository. The 2 'System reconnaissance' findings reference data in a JSON audit results file. The skill is a text-based Methods section reviewer with no code execution, network access, or external system interaction. No prompt injection attempts detected. Risk level: SAFE.

content.value_statement: Researchers often submit manuscripts with incomplete Methods sections that prevent other laboratories from replicating their work. This skill analyzes Methods text against a structured checklist to identify missing details, ambiguous descriptions, and reproducibility risks.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes Methods section text to identify missing replication-critical details
Classifies missing items as high or low priority based on impact on reproducibility
Detects ambiguous or underspecified descriptions in experimental procedures
Assigns a reproducibility risk rating with explicit rationale
Generates actionable supplementation suggestions mapped to specific gaps in the text
Provides structured output aligned with a reproducibility checklist when available
limitations: Cannot access external databases, journals, or websites to verify information
Cannot evaluate the scientific correctness of conclusions or statistical analyses
Cannot process non-textual content such as images, figures, or data files
Requires the full Methods section text provided by the user for accurate assessment

use_cases.title: Pre-submission Manuscript Self-Check
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a manuscript for journal submission uses this skill to verify their Methods section is complete before submission, reducing the risk of requests for additional information during peer review.

use_cases.title: Systematic Peer Review Assistance
use_cases.description: A reviewer evaluating another researcher's manuscript uses the skill to methodically identify missing methodological details, ambiguous procedures, and reproducibility concerns during the peer review process.

use_cases.title: Meta-Research Reproducibility Screening
use_cases.description: A research integrity officer screening multiple papers for reproducibility risks uses the skill to triage manuscripts by risk level, prioritizing those that need deeper methodological investigation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Methods Completeness Check
prompt_templates.prompt:
Please check whether the following Methods section is complete and reproducible. Identify missing items and unclear descriptions.

[PASTE METHODS SECTION HERE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick first-pass review of a Methods section for obvious gaps

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Methods Audit with Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt:
Please check whether the following Methods section is complete and reproducible. Identify missing items (high/low priority), unclear descriptions, and provide a reproducibility risk rating with suggestions. Use the structured checklist format.

[PASTE METHODS SECTION HERE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Thorough review using a structured checklist format with priority classification

prompt_templates.title: Statistical Methods Focus Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Please review the following Methods section with special attention to statistical methods. Check for sample size justification, randomization, blinding, statistical tests, multiple comparison corrections, effect sizes, and confidence interval reporting.

[PASTE METHODS SECTION HERE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review on statistical methodology and reporting completeness

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Section Reproducibility Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt:
Please check whether the following Methods section is complete and reproducible. Identify missing items (high/low priority), unclear descriptions, and provide a reproducibility risk rating with suggestions.

[PASTE METHODS SECTION HERE]

Optional context:
- Equipment list: [models, manufacturers]
- Software: [versions, packages]
- Statistical plan: [details]
- Data processing workflow: [steps]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive review with additional experimental context for maximum accuracy

output_examples.input: Methods: We used C57BL/6 mice for the experiment. Western blot was performed as previously described. Statistical analysis was done using GraphPad Prism.
output_examples.output:
## Reproducibility Check

### Missing Information (High Priority)
1. Animal details missing: age, sex, weight, and housing conditions of C57BL/6 mice
2. "Western blot performed as previously described" - no citation or protocol parameters
3. Antibody details missing: catalog numbers, clones, vendors, and lot numbers
4. Sample size not specified: number of mice per group not stated

### Missing Information (Low Priority)
1. GraphPad Prism version number not specified

### Reproducibility Risk Assessment
Risk Level: High
Rationale: Critical replication parameters (animal details, reagent identifiers, sample size) are absent. The phrase "as previously described" without a reference makes independent replication impossible.

output_examples.input: Methods: RNA was extracted from tissue samples using TRIzol reagent. qPCR was performed with SYBR Green on a Bio-Rad CFX96 system. Data were analyzed using the delta-delta Ct method.
output_examples.output:
## Reproducibility Check

### Missing Information (High Priority)
1. Tissue sample details missing: source, collection method, storage conditions
2. TRIzol reagent volume and protocol steps not specified
3. RNA quality assessment missing: concentration, purity ratios, integrity check method
4. qPCR cycling conditions not specified: temperatures, times, cycle numbers
5. Reference genes used for normalization not named

### Reproducibility Risk Assessment
Risk Level: Medium
Rationale: Key equipment (Bio-Rad CFX96) and general method (delta-delta Ct) are specified, but insufficient detail on sample processing, RNA quality, qPCR parameters, and normalization prevents confident replication.

best_practices: Provide the complete Methods section including supplementary protocols for the most thorough assessment.
Include specific details about equipment models, software versions, and reagent catalog numbers when available.
Use the reproducibility report as a revision checklist, addressing each identified gap before final submission.
anti_patterns: Submitting only the abstract or results section without the full Methods text, which prevents meaningful completeness analysis.
Asking the skill to verify scientific correctness or statistical validity of the described methods instead of completeness.
Requesting that the skill fabricate missing methodological details or access external sources for verification.

faq.question: What types of documents can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: This skill analyzes plain text, markdown, and content from the Methods section of a scientific paper. The text must be provided directly by the user.

faq.question: Can this skill verify if my methods are scientifically correct?
faq.answer: No. This skill checks for completeness and clarity of methodological descriptions. It cannot evaluate whether the described methods are scientifically valid or appropriate.

faq.question: Does this skill access external databases or journals?
faq.answer: No. This skill only processes text that the user explicitly provides. It does not connect to any external systems, databases, or websites.

faq.question: What is the reproducibility risk rating based on?
faq.answer: The rating is based on the presence and clarity of key methodological elements such as sample size, randomization, materials, equipment, and statistical methods.

faq.question: Can this skill check figures, images, or data files?
faq.answer: No. This skill can only analyze textual content. It cannot interpret images, figures, tables, or data files provided by the user.

faq.question: How is this skill different from human peer review?
faq.answer: This skill provides a structured completeness check but does not replace human peer review. Domain experts should verify the recommendations for accuracy and relevance.
